source_paper_title: Splitting Guarantees for Prophet Inequalities via Nonlinear Systems
context:
Section 6 (Final Remarks), page 28 (PDF page 28/39)

We leave the tightness of our approximation result in Theorem 2 through our nonlinear system (1)-(3) as an open question, i.e., whether \(\inf_{n\ge 1} \gamma_{n,k} = \sum_{\ell=1}^k \theta^\star_\ell\).
title: Equality of worst-case \,\((k,n)\)-SPI approximation ratio with the nonlinear-system constant sum \,\(\sum_{\ell=1}^k \theta^\star_\ell\)
background_latex:
Fix integers \(n\ge k\ge 1\). In the i.i.d. \((k,n)\)-selection prophet inequality (\((k,n)\)-SPI), one observes sequentially \(n\) i.i.d. nonnegative random variables \(X_1,\dots,X_n\sim F\) (known distribution) and must irrevocably accept at most \(k\) items, maximizing the expected sum of accepted values.

Let \(X_{(1)}\le \cdots\le X_{(n)}\) be the order statistics. The offline benchmark is
\[\mathrm{OPT}_{n,k}(F)=\sum_{j=n-k+1}^n \mathbb{E}[X_{(j)}].\]
Let \(A_{1,k}(F)\) denote the expected value of an optimal online policy for \((k,n)\)-SPI under \(F\). The optimal worst-case approximation ratio is
\[\gamma_{n,k}=\inf_F \frac{A_{1,k}(F)}{\mathrm{OPT}_{n,k}(F)},\]
where the infimum ranges over continuous distributions \(F\) supported on \(\mathbb{R}_+\).

For each fixed \(k\), the paper defines a system of \(k\) coupled nonlinear differential equations on \([0,1)\) (their equations (1)–(3)), involving unknown functions \(y_1,\dots,y_k\) with boundary conditions \(y_j(0)=1\) and \(\lim_{t\uparrow 1} y_j(t)=0\). This system is parameterized by constants \(\theta_1,\dots,\theta_k\ge 0\). Let \(\theta^\star=(\theta_1^\star,\dots,\theta_k^\star)\) denote the specific parameter vector for which the nonlinear system admits a solution (as established in the paper), and define \(S_k=\sum_{\ell=1}^k \theta_\ell^\star\).
problem_definition_latex:
Determine whether the nonlinear-system constant sum \(S_k=\sum_{\ell=1}^k \theta_\ell^\star\) equals the worst-case approximation ratio for \((k,n)\)-SPI in the sense that
\[\inf_{n\ge 1} \gamma_{n,k} \;=\; S_k.\]
Equivalently, prove or disprove that \(S_k\) is the exact optimal worst-case approximation factor (over all \(n\) and all i.i.d. nonnegative distributions \(F\)) for selecting at most \(k\) items online against the offline benchmark \(\mathrm{OPT}_{n,k}(F)\).

verification.notice: This entry was generated from the paper text only. It has not been independently verified as still open. If you believe the question is solved or incorrectly stated, please contact the maintainers.
